prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetheart, a CPU is like the little brain inside your computer or phone. It’s the part that does all the thinking and tells everything else what to do, just like how your brain tells your body to move or talk. It works really fast, even though it’s tiny—like a busy little bee buzzing around, making sure everything runs smoothly. Without it, your computer wouldn’t know how to open games or show pictures. It’s one of the most important pieces inside, even if you can’t see it! Now, let me get my glasses—I think I left them on the kitchen table again.
